description: Security findings, fixes, and hardening rules extracted from Cyber agent and QA audits

entries.title: JWT must use httpOnly cookies, never localStorage
entries.content: All pipeline runs that stored JWT in localStorage were flagged P0 by Cyber (S4) and QA (S8/S9). Rule: auth tokens go in httpOnly secure cookies with SameSite=Strict. Frontend must use auth context provider, never direct token access.

entries.title: HMAC for internal service-to-service auth
entries.content: Internal endpoints (points engine, event processing) must use HMAC-SHA256 with shared secret, not JWT. Prevent replay with timestamp window (300s max). Worker endpoints should be VPC-internal only (ALB internal listener).

entries.title: NestJS SetMetadata instead of Reflect.defineMetadata for RBAC
entries.content: Custom Roles decorator must use NestJS @SetMetadata() or Reflector.createDecorator(), NOT raw Reflect.defineMetadata(). The latter writes to descriptor.value which doesn't match context.getHandler() in guards, silently bypassing all role checks.

entries.title: Referral codes must use crypto.randomBytes, not Math.random
entries.content: Referral code generation used Math.random() (PRNG seeded by time), giving 36^8 entropy which is brutable. Use crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('base64url') for 128-bit entropy. Also rate-limit redemption to 10/min per staff_id.

entries.title: Referrer-Policy: no-referrer on all PWA endpoints
entries.content: PWA magic-link tokens appear in URL path. Without Referrer-Policy: no-referrer, tokens leak via HTTP Referer header to third-party resources. Set header globally for all /pwa/* routes.

entries.title: Points ledger must be append-only with recomputation for GDPR erasure
entries.content: Points balance uses append-only ledger pattern. GDPR erasure cannot simply DELETE rows — must anonymize client data while preserving aggregate integrity. Recompute balances after anonymization. new_balance field in response should come from ledger SUM, not cached value.

entries.title: Prototype pollution risk in fire-and-forget — validate keys with regex
entries.content: Functions using agentId/keys as JSON properties (e.g., recordAgentActivity) are vulnerable to __proto__ pollution. Fix: regex guard at entry: /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$/ before using as key. Mandatory for any module accepting user-supplied keys.

entries.title: Unauthenticated data endpoints leak system topology
entries.content: GET endpoints without auth (e.g., /api/agent-activity, /api/system/config) leak internal agent names, usage patterns, system structure. Not PII, but enables reconnaissance. Fix: add internal header check or localhost-only restriction. All admin endpoints need auth.

entries.title: File-based storage without size caps — DoS via unbounded growth
entries.content: Unlike logs-storage (MAX_ENTRIES=200), some stores have no cap. Attacker writes thousands of unique keys, bloating JSON file. Fix: add safety cap (if entries > N return). All file-based stores MUST have max entry count or file size limit.

entries.title: Rate limiting documented but not implemented = false security
entries.content: Endpoints have rate limit comments (60 req/min GET, 10 req/min POST) but zero code enforcement. Attacker floods file I/O. Fix: implement with @upstash/ratelimit sliding window. Return 429 with Retry-After header. Documentation != enforcement.

entries.title: POST endpoints without authentication = arbitrary data write
entries.content: POST /api/system/config has zero auth — anyone can overwrite config. Fix: validate CRON_SECRET header for backend-to-backend, getServerSession() for user-facing. Rule: ALL POST/PATCH/DELETE must authenticate. GET for non-PII can be public.

entries.title: Timestamp + user_id + IP = PII under 152-FZ
entries.content: Timestamp alone: not PII. With user_id + IP address: personal data per 152-FZ Article 3, requires Russian server localization. Store timestamp-only without IP by default. If IP logging added — requires Cyber review + 152-FZ compliance audit.
